lede: A look at October 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Georges Brassens and Ichiro Suzuki, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Australian Capital Territory
events.desc: The Australian Capital Territory becomes the first Australian jurisdiction to legalize same-sex marriage with the Marriage Equality (Same Sex) Act 2013.

events.title: Lance Armstrong
events.desc: Cyclist Lance Armstrong is formally stripped of his seven Tour de France titles after being charged for doping.

events.title: Bellview Airlines Flight 210
events.desc: Bellview Airlines Flight 210 crashes in Nigeria, killing all 117 people on board.

events.title: John Adams (composer)
events.desc: John Adams' opera Nixon in China premiered at the Houston Grand Opera.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: French resistance member Guy Môquet and 29 other hostages are executed by the Germans in retaliation for the death of a German officer.

events.title: Dod Orsborne
events.desc: Dod Orsborne, captain of the Girl Pat is convicted of its theft and imprisoned, having caused a media sensation when it went missing.

events.title: Blantyre mining disaster
events.desc: The Blantyre mining disaster in Scotland kills 207 miners.

events.title: Veneto
events.desc: A plebiscite ratifies the annexation of Veneto and Mantua to Italy, which had occurred three days before on October 19.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Schmücke dich, o liebe Seele (Adorn yourself, O dear soul) in Leipzig on the 20th Sunday after Trinity, based on the communion hymn of the same name.

events.title: Ahmad ibn Kayghalagh
events.desc: Abbasid general Ahmad ibn Kayghalagh leads a raid against the Byzantine Empire, taking 4,000–5,000 captives.

births.name: William IX, Duke of Aquitaine
births.line: Duke of Aquitaine and Gascony and Count of Poitou

births.name: Doris Lessing
births.line: British novelist, poet, playwright, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Helmut Krackowizer, Austrian motorcycle racer and journalist
Ernst von Fleischl-Marxow, Austrian physiologist and physician
Charles Martel, Frankish political and military leader

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pope John Paul II
